title: GenAI Security Crosswalk — Incident Database Schema
description: Schema for the crosswalk's incident view. data/incidents.json is generated from the upstream genai_incidents repo (single source of truth) by scripts/sync-incidents.mjs — do not hand-edit. Enrichment fields (maestro_layers, mitigations, impact) are optional and flow through from upstream as they are added there.

source.description: Provenance of this generated view (upstream repo, ref, tier, counts)

id.description: Upstream incident identifier (authoritative; kept verbatim from genai_incidents)

date.description: YYYY or YYYY-MM of incident discovery/disclosure

category.description: Confirmed real-world incident, researcher-demonstrated attack, red-team finding, or vulnerability disclosure

description.description: What happened, how it was discovered, and what the outcome was

owasp_entries.description: OWASP entry IDs this incident exemplifies (owasp_llm + owasp_asi + owasp_dsgai upstream)

mitre_atlas.description: MITRE ATLAS technique IDs (e.g. AML.T0051) — passthrough from upstream

mitre_atlas_tactics.description: MITRE ATLAS tactic IDs — passthrough from upstream

nist_ai_rmf.description: NIST AI RMF subcategory IDs (e.g. GOVERN-1.1) — passthrough from upstream

maestro_layers.description: MAESTRO architectural layer mapping (optional enrichment from upstream)

role.description: origin=where attack starts, propagation=how it spreads, impact=where harm manifests, blind-spot=where detection failed

impact.description: Actual or potential harm caused (optional enrichment from upstream)

mitigations.description: Controls that would prevent or contain this incident (optional enrichment from upstream)
